title: Flaky Umami Argentinian Squid Stew
description: A cozy, gluten‑free Argentinian squid stew bursting with umami, featuring tender, flaky squid bathed in a rich tomato and wine sauce.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil and blanch the prepared squid for 30 seconds, then drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat; add garlic and shallot, sauté until translucent, about 2 minutes.
Pour in the tomato puree and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Deglaze with the white wine, scraping up any browned bits, and reduce by half, about 5 minutes.
Add the clam juice or fish stock and bring to a gentle simmer; let it reduce for 10 minutes to concentrate the flavors.
Return the blanched squid to the pot, add smoked paprika and cumin, and simmer gently until the squid is just opaque, 2–3 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste; remove from heat.
Stir in chopped parsley for freshness and a pop of color.
Serve hot with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing over the stew.
